prose:
Method provenance and key results
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilicity), f⁻ (nucleophilicity), f⁰ (radical susceptibility), and the dual descriptor for nitrobenzene.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no RI density fitting) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz; single-point calculations on this fixed geometry for the N, N+1, and N–1 charge states (no re-optimization).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model requested).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation and anion states run as doublets (charge ±1, multiplicity 2) per default protocol.
Software: PySCF (backend invoked via chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill).
Convergence & integrity: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true.
Warnings (verbatim from result):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
No additional warnings were reported.

Selected numerical highlights (Mulliken scheme, atom indices 0-based; full arrays in output JSON):
Most electrophilic site  → atom 3 (C4)  f⁺ = 0.4964178
Most nucleophilic site   → atom 4 (C5)  f⁻ = 0.1072882
Dual descriptor highest  → atom 3 (C4)  Δf = 0.51070983

Reproducibility
CLI command (abbreviated):
  chemkit fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 … nitrobenzene.xyz --out …/live.json
